While relationship management with vendors requires human interaction, the core analytical and administrative functions are highly susceptible to AI augmentation, particularly as Pakistan's retail sector increasingly digitizes its procurement processes.

scores.title: Hotel Receptionist
scores.rationale: Hotel receptionists perform a mix of routine administrative tasks (booking management, billing) that AI can assist with, but the core role requires real-time human interaction with guests for check-ins, problem resolution, and hospitality services. In Pakistan's hospitality sector, personal service and human warmth remain highly valued by guests, and many hotels still rely on traditional systems rather than fully digital operations, limiting AI's immediate impact on the profession.

scores.title: Chef / Cook
scores.rationale: Cooking is fundamentally a hands-on craft requiring physical skills, sensory judgment (taste, smell, texture), and real-time adaptation that AI cannot replicate. While AI might assist with recipe suggestions or inventory management in high-end restaurants, the core work of food preparation, cooking techniques, and quality control requires human presence and manual dexterity that remains largely unaffected by AI automation in Pakistan's hospitality sector.

scores.title: Waiter
scores.rationale: Waiters perform primarily physical and interpersonal work requiring real-time human presence - taking orders, serving food, handling customer interactions, and managing table service. While AI might assist with peripheral tasks like order processing or inventory management in more digitized restaurants, the core job functions require physical presence and human interaction that cannot be automated. In Pakistan's hospitality sector, which relies heavily on personal service and has limited adoption of advanced restaurant technologies, AI exposure remains minimal.

scores.title: Tour Guide
scores.rationale: Tour guides primarily provide in-person experiences, storytelling, and real-time interaction with tourists in physical locations, which are core human activities that AI cannot replicate. While AI tools might assist with research, itinerary planning, or translation apps, the essence of guiding tourists through Pakistan's cultural sites, managing group dynamics, and providing personalized local insights requires human presence and cultural knowledge that remains largely irreplaceable.

scores.title: Hotel Manager
scores.rationale: Hotel management involves a significant mix of digital tasks (revenue management, booking systems, financial analysis) that AI can enhance, alongside essential interpersonal responsibilities requiring physical presence like guest relations, staff supervision, and crisis management. In Pakistan's hospitality sector, while larger hotels are adopting digital management systems that AI can optimize, the role fundamentally requires human judgment for customer service, cultural sensitivity, and on-site leadership that cannot be fully automated.

scores.title: Housekeeper (Hotel)
scores.rationale: Hotel housekeeping is fundamentally physical work requiring manual cleaning, bed-making, room maintenance, and real-time problem-solving in unpredictable environments. While AI might assist with minor scheduling or inventory management tasks, the core job of physically cleaning and maintaining hotel rooms cannot be automated with current technology. Pakistan's hospitality sector remains labor-intensive with limited adoption of advanced automation technologies.

scores.title: Travel Agent
scores.rationale: Travel agents primarily work with digital systems - booking flights, hotels, researching destinations, and communicating with clients online. AI tools can already handle most core functions like finding optimal routes, comparing prices, and making reservations, while chatbots can provide 24/7 customer service. In Pakistan's growing digital economy, online travel platforms and AI-powered booking systems are rapidly replacing traditional travel agents, especially as internet penetration increases and customers become more comfortable with self-service digital tools.

scores.title: Fast Food Worker
scores.rationale: Fast food work in Pakistan is predominantly physical and interpersonal - taking orders face-to-face, preparing food by hand, cleaning, and providing customer service in person. While AI might assist with order management systems or inventory tracking in larger chains, the core job functions require physical presence and manual tasks that cannot be digitized, especially given Pakistan's preference for fresh, locally-prepared food and high-touch customer service.

scores.title: Baker
scores.rationale: Baking is fundamentally a hands-on craft requiring physical skills like kneading dough, operating ovens, and real-time sensory judgment for texture, temperature, and timing that cannot be digitized. While AI might assist with minor tasks like inventory management or recipe optimization, the core work of mixing, shaping, and baking remains entirely manual and requires human presence throughout the process.

scores.title: Journalist / Reporter
scores.rationale: Journalism is predominantly knowledge work involving research, writing, and digital communication - all areas where AI tools are rapidly advancing and already being adopted by Pakistani media outlets for content generation, fact-checking, and translation. While human judgment, source relationships, and field reporting remain important, AI can significantly enhance productivity in core tasks like writing, editing, and data analysis, making this occupation highly exposed to AI transformation in Pakistan's increasingly digital media landscape.

scores.title: TV Anchor / News Presenter
scores.rationale: TV anchors combine digital content creation (script writing, research) with essential on-camera presence and real-time audience interaction that requires human charisma and credibility. While AI can assist with research, teleprompter content, and background preparation, the core function of delivering news with authority and connecting with Pakistani audiences requires human presence and cultural understanding. Pakistan's media landscape still heavily values recognizable human personalities, though AI tools for content preparation and fact-checking are increasingly useful.

scores.title: Cameraman / Videographer
scores.rationale: Cameramen/videographers require physical presence at shoots, manual operation of equipment, and real-time creative decision-making that AI cannot replicate. However, AI is increasingly automating post-production tasks like editing, color correction, and basic video enhancement, which may reduce demand for some technical roles. In Pakistan's growing media sector, the core filming work remains human-dependent, but AI tools for video processing are becoming more accessible even with limited digital infrastructure.

scores.title: Radio Host / RJ
scores.rationale: Radio hosts face moderate-high AI exposure as their core work involves content creation, script writing, and voice-based communication - areas where AI is rapidly advancing. While live interaction with callers and real-time audience engagement provide some protection, AI can already assist with content research, script generation, and even voice synthesis. Pakistan's growing digital media landscape and the shift toward podcast-style content increases exposure, though traditional radio's continued popularity in local languages may provide some buffer against full automation.

scores.title: Content Writer / Copywriter
scores.rationale: Content writing and copywriting are entirely digital occupations where AI tools like ChatGPT and Claude can already produce high-quality written content across multiple formats and languages, including Urdu. Pakistan's large freelance writing community, which serves both domestic and international markets, faces particularly high disruption as clients can increasingly use AI for content generation at a fraction of the cost.

scores.title: Social Media Manager
scores.rationale: Social media management is predominantly digital work involving content creation, strategy development, analytics interpretation, and audience engagement - all areas where AI tools are rapidly advancing and already being adopted. In Pakistan's growing digital economy with its large freelance workforce and expanding social media presence, AI can automate content generation, optimize posting schedules, analyze engagement metrics, and even handle basic customer interactions, making individual managers significantly more productive while potentially reducing overall demand for human managers.

scores.title: Video Editor
scores.rationale: Video editing is fundamentally digital work done entirely on computers, making it highly susceptible to AI automation and enhancement tools. AI video editing capabilities are rapidly advancing with automated cutting, color correction, effects generation, and even AI-generated content, which directly impacts the core tasks of video editors. Pakistan's growing digital media sector and large freelance workforce in creative services makes this occupation particularly vulnerable to AI disruption.

scores.title: Translator / Interpreter
scores.rationale: Translation work is fundamentally digital and language-based, making it highly susceptible to AI tools like ChatGPT and specialized translation models that are rapidly improving in quality. Pakistan's large freelance translation workforce, particularly those serving international clients online, faces significant disruption as AI translation becomes more accurate and cost-effective. While human translators may still be needed for nuanced, cultural, or specialized content, the volume of routine translation work available will likely decrease substantially.

scores.title: Public Relations Officer
scores.rationale: Public Relations Officers work primarily in digital domains - writing press releases, managing social media, creating content, and analyzing media coverage - all areas where AI tools are rapidly advancing and already providing significant productivity gains. While relationship-building and strategic communication require human judgment, the core deliverables (content creation, media monitoring, campaign analysis) are increasingly AI-assisted, making this a high-exposure occupation in Pakistan's growing digital media landscape.

scores.title: Advertising Executive
scores.rationale: Advertising executives work primarily in digital domains - creating campaigns, analyzing data, managing client communications, and developing creative strategies that can be significantly enhanced by AI tools for content generation, audience targeting, and performance optimization. While client relationship management and strategic thinking still require human judgment, the core creative and analytical work is increasingly AI-augmented, making workers more productive but potentially reducing overall demand in Pakistan's growing digital advertising sector.

scores.title: Lawyer / Advocate
scores.rationale: Legal work in Pakistan involves substantial knowledge work that AI can assist with - research, document drafting, case analysis, and contract review - making lawyers significantly more productive. However, the profession retains essential human elements including court appearances, client counseling, negotiation, and navigating Pakistan's complex legal system that blends common law, Islamic law, and local practices. While AI tools are already transforming legal research and document preparation globally, Pakistan's legal system still heavily relies on physical court proceedings and relationship-based practice.

scores.title: Judge
scores.rationale: While AI can assist judges with legal research, case analysis, and document review, the core judicial functions require human judgment, interpretation of law, courtroom presence, and complex decision-making that considers social context and equity. In Pakistan's legal system, where oral arguments, witness testimony, and nuanced understanding of local customs are crucial, the human element remains irreplaceable despite AI's potential as a research and analytical tool.

scores.title: Legal Advisor / Corporate Counsel
scores.rationale: Legal advisory work involves substantial knowledge processing - contract analysis, legal research, document drafting, and regulatory compliance - all areas where AI tools are becoming highly capable. While client relationships and courtroom advocacy require human judgment, much of corporate counsel work involves analyzing documents and providing written advice that AI can increasingly assist with or automate. Pakistan's growing corporate sector and digitization of legal processes will accelerate AI adoption in this field.

scores.title: Paralegal / Legal Assistant
scores.rationale: Paralegals perform predominantly digital knowledge work including legal research, document drafting, case preparation, and client communication - all areas where AI tools are rapidly advancing and already showing significant capabilities. While some client interaction and court appearances require human presence, the core tasks of legal research, document review, and administrative support are highly susceptible to AI automation and augmentation. In Pakistan's legal sector, which is increasingly digitizing case management and research processes, AI tools can substantially enhance paralegal productivity or potentially reduce demand for traditional paralegal roles.

scores.title: Notary Public / Deed Writer
scores.rationale: Notary Public/Deed Writer work involves standardized document preparation, verification, and legal formatting - tasks that are increasingly digitizable and where AI can assist with template generation, error checking, and routine legal language. While the role requires some human judgment for verification and client interaction, the core work product is digital documents following established legal formats, making it highly susceptible to AI automation in Pakistan's growing digital documentation ecosystem.

scores.title: Power Plant Operator
scores.rationale: Power plant operators primarily perform hands-on technical work monitoring equipment, conducting physical inspections, and responding to real-time operational conditions that require human presence and judgment. While AI may assist with data analysis and predictive maintenance in Pakistan's more modern power facilities, the core responsibilities involve physical system control, safety protocols, and emergency response that cannot be automated. Pakistan's power infrastructure varies widely in technological sophistication, with many plants still requiring substantial manual oversight and intervention.

scores.title: Solar Panel Installer / Technician
scores.rationale: Solar panel installation and maintenance is predominantly physical work requiring hands-on technical skills, working at heights, and real-time problem-solving in outdoor environments. While AI might assist with system diagnostics or scheduling, the core job involves manual installation, wiring, and on-site troubleshooting that cannot be automated. Pakistan's growing solar sector will likely increase demand for these skilled technicians rather than reduce it through AI automation.

scores.title: Petroleum Engineer
scores.rationale: Petroleum engineers combine significant digital analysis work (reservoir modeling, production optimization, data analysis) with field operations requiring physical presence at drilling sites and facilities. While AI can enhance reservoir simulation, predictive maintenance, and production forecasting - areas where Pakistan's oil and gas sector is increasingly digitizing - the job still requires substantial on-site engineering judgment, safety oversight, and coordination with field crews that cannot be fully automated.

scores.title: Gas Fitter
scores.rationale: Gas fitting is primarily hands-on technical work requiring physical installation, maintenance, and repair of gas systems, with safety-critical tasks that demand human expertise and real-time problem-solving in varied physical environments. While AI might assist with minor administrative tasks like scheduling or accessing technical manuals, the core work involves manual skills, safety protocols, and on-site troubleshooting that cannot be digitized. In Pakistan's context, where gas infrastructure often requires customized solutions and direct human intervention, the physical and safety-critical nature of this trade provides strong protection against AI displacement.

scores.title: Meter Reader
scores.rationale: Meter readers primarily perform physical tasks requiring them to visit customer locations, navigate neighborhoods, and manually read utility meters - work that fundamentally requires human presence in the field. While digital meter reading technology and smart meters could reduce demand for this occupation over time, Pakistan's utility infrastructure remains largely traditional with manual meters, and the physical nature of accessing meters in diverse residential and commercial settings creates natural barriers to AI automation.

scores.title: Coal Miner
scores.rationale: Coal mining is fundamentally physical work requiring human presence in dangerous underground environments, manual operation of heavy machinery, and real-time safety decisions that cannot be digitized. While AI might assist with some surface-level planning or safety monitoring systems, the core extraction work remains entirely hands-on and location-dependent, making it largely immune to AI displacement in Pakistan's mining context.

scores.title: Quarry Worker
scores.rationale: Quarry workers perform physically demanding manual labor including rock extraction, heavy machinery operation, and material handling that requires human presence in hazardous environments. The work is entirely hands-on and location-specific, with minimal digital components that AI could impact. Pakistan's quarrying sector remains largely traditional and labor-intensive, with limited technology adoption that would create pathways for AI integration.

scores.title: Mining Engineer
scores.rationale: Mining engineers combine significant digital work (geological modeling, mine planning software, data analysis) with essential field presence for site inspections, safety oversight, and equipment management. While AI can enhance geological surveys, predictive maintenance, and optimization algorithms, the core responsibilities require physical presence at mining sites and real-time decision-making in hazardous environments. Pakistan's mining sector, though growing, has moderate digitization levels which limits immediate AI adoption compared to more advanced mining operations globally.

scores.title: Gem Cutter / Stone Worker
scores.rationale: Gem cutting and stone work requires highly specialized manual dexterity, tactile feedback, and years of hands-on experience to assess stone quality, grain patterns, and cutting angles that cannot be replicated by AI. In Pakistan's traditional gem and jewelry industry, particularly in areas like Peshawar and Quetta, this craft relies on artisanal skills passed down through generations and direct physical manipulation of materials that AI cannot perform.

scores.title: Domestic Worker / Maid
scores.rationale: Domestic workers perform entirely physical, hands-on tasks like cleaning, cooking, and childcare that require human presence in homes. In Pakistan's context, this occupation remains largely informal with minimal technology integration, and the personal trust relationship between employers and domestic workers cannot be replicated by AI systems.

scores.title: Guard / Chowkidar
scores.rationale: Guards/chowkidars perform primarily physical security work requiring real-time human presence, vigilance, and immediate response to threats or emergencies. While basic digital tools like CCTV monitoring might be enhanced by AI, the core function of physical security and deterrence cannot be automated, especially in Pakistan's context where personal security services rely heavily on human judgment and local knowledge. The job's low education requirements and emphasis on physical presence make it largely immune to AI disruption.

scores.title: Security Guard (Private)
scores.rationale: Security guards perform primarily physical surveillance and protection duties that require real-time human presence, situational awareness, and the ability to respond to unpredictable security threats. While some modern security systems incorporate AI-powered cameras and monitoring tools, the core function of providing human security presence, making judgment calls, and physically responding to incidents cannot be automated. In Pakistan's context, most private security work involves basic physical guarding of premises, gates, and properties with minimal digital infrastructure.

scores.title: Driver (Private)
scores.rationale: Private drivers perform entirely physical work requiring real-time navigation, vehicle operation, and human judgment in unpredictable traffic conditions - skills that AI cannot replicate without autonomous vehicles. While Pakistan has limited autonomous vehicle infrastructure and adoption, the core driving task remains fundamentally manual and location-dependent, making this occupation minimally exposed to AI disruption in the foreseeable future.

scores.title: Barber / Hairdresser
scores.rationale: Barbing and hairdressing require hands-on manual skills, physical dexterity, and real-time interaction with clients in person. The core work involves cutting, styling, and grooming hair through direct physical manipulation that cannot be digitized or automated by AI. In Pakistan's context, this occupation remains firmly rooted in traditional service delivery with minimal digital integration, making it highly resistant to AI disruption.

scores.title: Beautician
scores.rationale: Beautician work is predominantly hands-on, requiring physical skills like hair cutting, styling, makeup application, and skincare treatments that demand real-time human interaction and manual dexterity. While AI might assist with minor tasks like appointment scheduling or trend research, the core services require physical presence and personalized human touch that cannot be digitized. In Pakistan's context, most beauty services operate in small salons or home-based setups with limited digital infrastructure, further reducing AI's potential impact on daily operations.

scores.title: Laundry Worker / Dhobi
scores.rationale: Laundry work in Pakistan is predominantly manual and physical, involving washing, drying, ironing, and handling clothes by hand or with basic equipment. The work requires physical presence, manual dexterity, and direct handling of garments, with most dhobis operating in informal settings with minimal digital infrastructure. While AI might eventually impact industrial laundry facilities, the traditional dhobi profession remains largely unchanged by digital technologies.

scores.title: Sweeper / Sanitary Worker
scores.rationale: Sweeping and sanitary work is entirely physical labor requiring human presence in varied, unpredictable environments across Pakistan's diverse urban and rural settings. The work involves manual cleaning, waste collection, and maintenance tasks that cannot be digitized or automated with current AI technology. While some developed countries are experimenting with robotic cleaning systems, Pakistan's infrastructure limitations, cost constraints, and the informal nature of most sanitary work make AI adoption virtually impossible in this sector.

scores.title: Gardener / Mali
scores.rationale: Gardening work is fundamentally physical and hands-on, requiring direct interaction with plants, soil, and outdoor environments that cannot be digitized or automated by AI. In Pakistan's context, most gardeners work in residential settings or small-scale operations with minimal technology adoption, making AI tools largely irrelevant to their core tasks of planting, watering, pruning, and maintaining gardens.

scores.title: Nanny / Childcare Worker
scores.rationale: Childcare work is fundamentally about physical presence, emotional connection, and real-time human interaction with children - tasks that AI cannot replicate. In Pakistan's context, where childcare is highly personal and family-oriented, the human element of nurturing, safety supervision, and building trust with families remains irreplaceable by digital tools.

scores.title: Cobbler / Shoe Repairer
scores.rationale: Cobbler work is entirely hands-on, requiring manual dexterity to repair shoes, stitch leather, and work with physical materials and tools. In Pakistan's context, most cobblers operate small roadside stalls or shops serving local communities, with minimal digitization and no computer-based work components that AI could impact.

scores.title: Tailor (Local)
scores.rationale: Tailoring is fundamentally a hands-on craft requiring physical manipulation of fabric, precise measurements of individual customers, and manual sewing skills that cannot be digitized. While AI might assist with basic pattern design or inventory management, the core work of fitting, cutting, and sewing garments remains entirely physical and requires human dexterity and judgment. In Pakistan's context, most local tailors operate in traditional settings with minimal digital infrastructure, serving customers who value personal fitting and customization.

scores.title: Mechanic (Auto)
scores.rationale: Auto mechanics work primarily with physical components, requiring hands-on diagnostic skills, manual dexterity, and real-time problem-solving with mechanical systems. While AI might assist with computerized diagnostic tools in modern vehicles, Pakistan's automotive landscape includes many older vehicles and the core work remains fundamentally physical - disassembling, repairing, and reassembling mechanical parts. The informal nature of many auto repair shops in Pakistan and limited digitization further reduces AI's immediate impact on daily work.

scores.title: Mechanic (Motorcycle)
scores.rationale: Motorcycle mechanics in Pakistan perform predominantly hands-on physical work - diagnosing engine problems through sound and touch, disassembling parts, welding, and testing repairs that require real-time problem-solving in unpredictable mechanical situations. While AI might eventually assist with diagnostic tools or parts ordering, the core work of physically repairing motorcycles in Pakistan's numerous small workshops remains largely unchanged, especially given the informal nature of most motorcycle repair businesses and limited digital infrastructure adoption.

scores.title: AC / Refrigeration Technician
scores.rationale: AC/Refrigeration technicians perform primarily hands-on, physical work requiring manual skills, diagnostic expertise, and on-site presence to repair and maintain cooling systems. While AI might assist with minor tasks like scheduling appointments or accessing repair manuals, the core job involves physical troubleshooting, component replacement, and real-time problem-solving in Pakistan's diverse residential and commercial settings. The work cannot be digitized or performed remotely, providing strong protection against AI displacement.

scores.title: Mobile Phone Repair Technician
scores.rationale: Mobile phone repair is primarily hands-on technical work requiring physical manipulation of small components, soldering, and diagnostic skills that must be performed in person. While AI might assist with some diagnostic software or parts identification, the core work involves manual dexterity and real-world problem-solving that cannot be digitized. In Pakistan's context, this trade remains largely informal and relies on practical experience rather than digital tools.

scores.title: Musician / Singer
scores.rationale: Musicians and singers primarily engage in creative performance that requires human artistic expression, emotional connection with audiences, and physical presence for live performances - core elements that AI cannot replicate. While AI tools can assist with composition, production, and distribution, the fundamental value of music lies in human creativity and live performance, which remain largely protected from automation in Pakistan's music industry that heavily emphasizes live concerts, wedding performances, and cultural events.

scores.title: Actor / Performer
scores.rationale: Acting and performing are fundamentally human-centered activities requiring physical presence, emotional expression, and real-time audience interaction that cannot be digitized. While AI may assist with some peripheral tasks like script analysis or scheduling, and could potentially impact content creation in Pakistan's growing digital media sector, the core craft of live performance and on-camera acting remains inherently human and requires physical embodiment that AI cannot replicate.

scores.title: Painter / Fine Artist
scores.rationale: Fine artists and painters primarily engage in physical, hands-on creative work that requires manual dexterity, artistic vision, and direct manipulation of physical materials like canvas, paint, and brushes. While AI image generation tools are emerging, the core artistic process remains deeply human-centered, involving tactile skills and personal creative expression that cannot be easily replicated. In Pakistan's context, the art market is still largely traditional with limited digital integration, and most fine artists work with physical mediums rather than digital platforms.

scores.title: Calligrapher
scores.rationale: Calligraphy work is increasingly digital, with AI tools now capable of generating sophisticated calligraphic designs and fonts that can replicate traditional styles. In Pakistan's growing digital design market, clients may increasingly opt for AI-generated calligraphy for cost and speed, though demand for authentic handwritten work for religious texts and ceremonial purposes may provide some protection. The occupation's core creative output can be digitized and automated, making it highly vulnerable to AI disruption.

scores.title: Cricket Player (Professional)
scores.rationale: Professional cricket is fundamentally a physical sport requiring real-time athletic performance, hand-eye coordination, and in-person competition that cannot be digitized or automated. While AI may assist with peripheral activities like performance analysis, training optimization, or match strategy, the core job of playing cricket remains entirely dependent on human physical and mental skills in unpredictable game situations. Pakistan's strong cricket culture and the sport's inherently human nature provide natural barriers to AI displacement.

scores.title: Sports Coach
scores.rationale: Sports coaching is fundamentally about real-time human interaction, physical demonstration, and in-person motivation during training and competitions. While AI tools might assist with performance analytics, video analysis, or training schedules, the core work requires physical presence, emotional intelligence, and adaptive interpersonal skills that cannot be digitized. In Pakistan's sports culture, the mentor-athlete relationship and hands-on guidance remain essential elements that AI cannot replicate.

scores.title: Photographer
scores.rationale: Photography involves both technical digital skills (editing, post-processing, digital asset management) and creative/interpersonal elements (client interaction, artistic vision, on-location shooting). While AI tools are rapidly advancing in image generation, editing, and enhancement - making photographers more productive but also creating competition - the profession still requires human creativity, client relationships, and physical presence for events, portraits, and specialized shoots. In Pakistan's context, wedding photography and event coverage remain highly valued services requiring human presence, though the growing digital freelance market faces increasing AI competition.

scores.title: Event Planner
scores.rationale: Event planning involves a significant mix of digital coordination tasks (vendor management, scheduling, budgeting, marketing) that AI can assist with, alongside essential human elements like client relationship building, creative vision, and on-site execution. In Pakistan's context, while the growing corporate and wedding event industry increasingly uses digital tools for planning and promotion, the core value remains in personal relationships, cultural understanding, and physical presence during events, limiting AI's ability to fully transform the role.

scores.title: NGO Field Worker
scores.rationale: NGO field workers in Pakistan primarily engage in community outreach, direct service delivery, and face-to-face interactions with beneficiaries in rural and urban areas, requiring physical presence and cultural sensitivity that AI cannot replicate. While AI might assist with basic data collection, reporting, or scheduling tasks, the core work involves building trust with communities, conducting field visits, and implementing programs that require human judgment and local context understanding. Pakistan's NGO sector relies heavily on personal relationships and on-ground presence, making this occupation largely resistant to AI disruption.

scores.title: Social Worker
scores.rationale: Social work in Pakistan involves extensive face-to-face interaction with vulnerable communities, field visits, and building trust relationships that require human empathy and cultural understanding. While AI might assist with case documentation and report writing, the core functions of counseling, community mobilization, and direct service delivery in Pakistan's diverse social contexts remain fundamentally human-centered. The profession's reliance on interpersonal skills and physical presence in communities provides strong protection against AI displacement.

scores.title: Community Health Worker
scores.rationale: Community Health Workers in Pakistan primarily engage in direct human interaction, home visits, health education, and basic medical screenings that require physical presence and interpersonal trust-building in local communities. While AI might assist with basic record-keeping or health information lookup, the core functions of community outreach, culturally sensitive health education, and building relationships with families cannot be digitized. The role's effectiveness depends heavily on local language skills, cultural understanding, and face-to-face human connection, which are fundamental barriers to AI replacement.

scores.title: Development Program Manager
scores.rationale: Development Program Managers work primarily with digital tools for planning, monitoring, reporting, and stakeholder communication - areas where AI can significantly enhance productivity through automated report generation, data analysis, and proposal writing. However, the role requires substantial human judgment for community engagement, relationship building with donors and beneficiaries, and navigating Pakistan's complex socio-political landscape. While AI can streamline administrative tasks and improve decision-making through better data insights, the core interpersonal and contextual aspects of development work in Pakistan's diverse communities still require human expertise.

scores.title: Monitoring & Evaluation Officer
scores.rationale: M&E Officers primarily work with data analysis, report writing, survey design, and performance measurement - all digital tasks where AI tools can significantly enhance productivity through automated data processing, visualization, and report generation. While the role requires some field visits and stakeholder engagement, the core work of analyzing program effectiveness and creating evaluation frameworks is increasingly supported by AI-powered analytics tools that are becoming accessible even in Pakistan's development sector.

scores.title: Human Rights Activist
scores.rationale: Human rights activism involves substantial fieldwork, community engagement, and real-time advocacy that requires physical presence and deep cultural understanding of Pakistan's diverse communities. While AI can assist with research, report writing, and data analysis tasks, the core work of building trust with vulnerable populations, conducting interviews, and navigating Pakistan's complex social and political landscape requires human judgment and interpersonal skills that AI cannot replicate.

scores.title: HR Manager / Officer
scores.rationale: HR management involves substantial knowledge work including recruitment screening, policy development, performance analysis, and employee communications - all areas where AI tools can significantly enhance productivity. While the role requires human judgment for complex employee relations and strategic decisions, much of the routine HR work (resume screening, scheduling, basic queries, report generation) can be automated or AI-assisted. In Pakistan's banking sector, which is increasingly digitized, HR managers are likely to adopt AI tools for efficiency gains, though the interpersonal aspects of the role provide some protection from complete automation.

scores.title: Marketing Manager
scores.rationale: Marketing managers work primarily with digital tools for campaign development, data analysis, content creation, and customer insights - all areas where AI is rapidly advancing and already providing significant productivity gains. While the role requires strategic thinking and stakeholder management that benefits from human judgment, the core work products (marketing strategies, content, analytics reports) are fundamentally digital and AI-assistable. In Pakistan's growing digital marketing landscape, AI tools for social media management, customer segmentation, and campaign optimization are becoming increasingly accessible and valuable.

scores.title: Office Clerk / Assistant
scores.rationale: Office clerks perform predominantly digital tasks like data entry, document processing, filing, and basic administrative work that AI can increasingly automate or significantly enhance. In Pakistan's government sector, while digitization is still progressing, the routine and repetitive nature of clerical work makes it highly susceptible to AI tools for document management, data processing, and administrative automation. The combination of standardized processes and digital workflow adoption in Pakistani government offices creates substantial exposure to AI-driven productivity gains.

scores.title: Data Entry Operator
scores.rationale: Data entry is purely routine information processing work done entirely on computers, with no physical or creative components - exactly the type of work AI excels at automating. In Pakistan's IT sector, automated data processing tools and AI systems can already perform most data entry tasks faster and more accurately than humans. This occupation faces immediate and complete disruption as businesses adopt readily available AI solutions to replace manual data entry work.

scores.title: Receptionist
scores.rationale: Receptionists handle a mix of routine information processing (scheduling, data entry, basic inquiries) that AI can increasingly automate, alongside interpersonal tasks requiring human presence for greeting visitors and handling complex situations. In Pakistan's context, while many businesses still rely on human receptionists for cultural reasons and the personal touch valued in service interactions, the digital transformation of appointment systems and customer service through chatbots is gradually reducing demand for traditional receptionist roles.

scores.title: Call Center Agent
scores.rationale: Call center work is fundamentally digital communication that can be increasingly handled by AI chatbots and voice assistants, which are rapidly improving in natural language processing capabilities. Pakistan's large call center industry, serving both domestic and international markets, is particularly vulnerable as companies seek cost savings through AI automation. While human agents may still be needed for complex issues, AI will likely handle routine inquiries, significantly reducing workforce demand.

scores.title: Project Manager
scores.rationale: IT project management involves substantial digital work including planning, coordination, reporting, and stakeholder communication that AI tools can significantly enhance or automate. While human judgment and relationship management remain important, AI can already handle many core tasks like scheduling, risk analysis, progress tracking, and documentation. Pakistan's growing IT sector and remote work culture make this occupation particularly susceptible to AI-driven productivity gains that could reduce demand for traditional project managers.

scores.title: Supply Chain Manager
scores.rationale: Supply chain management involves substantial digital work including data analysis, demand forecasting, vendor coordination, and logistics optimization - all areas where AI tools can significantly enhance productivity. While the role requires some human judgment for relationship management and strategic decisions, the core analytical and planning functions are increasingly supported by AI-powered supply chain software. In Pakistan's growing e-commerce and manufacturing sectors, companies are rapidly adopting digital supply chain solutions, making managers who leverage AI tools much more effective than those who don't.

scores.title: Import / Export Agent
scores.rationale: Import/Export Agents handle substantial documentation, regulatory compliance, and logistics coordination that can be significantly enhanced by AI tools for customs processing, trade documentation, and supply chain optimization. While the role requires relationship management with clients and suppliers, the core work involves information processing, regulatory tracking, and communication that AI can streamline. Pakistan's growing digital trade infrastructure and e-commerce sector make AI adoption increasingly viable for trade facilitation tasks.

scores.title: Entrepreneur / Small Business Owner
scores.rationale: Entrepreneurs and small business owners in Pakistan's retail/wholesale sector face moderate AI exposure as their work combines digital tasks (inventory management, accounting, marketing) with essential human elements like relationship building, negotiation, and physical operations. While AI tools can assist with bookkeeping, customer analysis, and online marketing - particularly valuable for Pakistan's growing e-commerce sector - the core entrepreneurial functions of strategic decision-making, supplier relationships, and local market navigation require human judgment and cultural understanding. Pakistan's predominantly cash-based, relationship-driven business culture and limited digital infrastructure in many areas provide some protection against full AI disruption.

scores.title: Freelancer (General)
scores.rationale: Freelancers in Pakistan's IT sector primarily work in digital domains like web development, graphic design, content writing, and digital marketing - all areas where AI tools are rapidly advancing and already being integrated into workflows. Pakistan's large freelance workforce, which heavily relies on platforms like Upwork and Fiverr for international clients, faces significant disruption as AI can now perform many routine technical tasks that previously required human freelancers, though skilled freelancers who adapt to use AI tools may become more productive.
